title: U.S. (Count the Guns!) (Joke)

[[File:Amajon.png|300px|center|link=]]
Add to Cart
==Description== U.S. is thought to be a sentient program that purchases guns off of any website it can find. After buying said guns, it will then leave a review regarding the guns it bought. It has been recorded that U.S. has the ability to modify the properties of the guns it buys and inserts reviews about the modified version. It is unknown whether or not U.S. can modify all guns, or if it is limited to just the guns it buys. U.S.'s intelligence level is unknown, as it has written reviews about the guns it buys either perfectly accurate to the original product, but has also written reviews that detail features that have not been implemented in the product. U.S. is believed to have no physical body and is instead a digital manifestation. U.S. is capable of creating user profiles to then comment on products. The reviews created by U.S. seem to have the ability to modify the products themselves, though many believe this to simply be the entity to have a clairvoyant ability. The true reason remains unknown. ==Behavior== When discovered, U.S. will engage in threatening behavior towards those that discovered it. It will only stop this once its user profile gets forcefully deleted. It always appears to know the __cpLocations of those it is threatening, though nothing will ever happen to those it sends threats to. If one threatens U.S. back, U.S. will start to exhibit extreme behavior and even start to make threats to itself, to the point of threats of suicide. U.S.'s threats that it sends are always consistent with their involvement with guns. U.S. will always threaten the user with the gun featured on the page that it commented on. ==Discovery== The first known documentation of U.S. was on July 4th, 2000, on a spin-off of Amazon. On the site, U.S. purchased a gun worth 400.57 USD, only to comment "It was the worst thing I have ever come into contact with". Four hours later, the creator of the gun posted a hate comment towards U.S., only for U.S. to start threatening the creator. The creator was later kidnapped by an unknown group of people.

=Count the Guns!= {{Underline|ffffff}}
Count the guns, for bodies litter the ground with their dull, crimson-red glow. With each homicide a man ascends above with either a smile or a frown. Or they may fall. Falling with a frown of a thousand frowns. Death is coming for you. It hides and prowls around you. The unsuspecting victim. As flowers turn black, withering beyond recognition, escape is unlikely. This is it. Give yourself up. The facade is upon you. Let it engulf you. This is your crimson-red end. Fate cannot deny it. It is against you. All good things must end. And that includes you.
{{Underline|ffffff}}
I was never a good person. I always killed people for fun. When I started, I don't know. I think it was when I killed my parents. Both abused me too much. CPS never would've helped me. We lived in a remote place in the middle of nowhere. I think it was in America, but I could never tell you. I walked outside of my makeshift house. I looked up at the sky. There, stars shined brighter than the sun. Oh, how they marveled in the wake of my presence. I thought about all that I had done. All that was wrong. I had evaded the law for much too long. It was time I turned myself in. I walked forever. How long I walked for, I could not tell you. It was like Hell. I trudged through the desert, waiting for the station to come into view. But it never did. I started to wonder if I truly lived that far from society. It was plausible. I eventually found a house. It was empty. No one was inside. I opened the door. Everything worked fine. I stayed the night there, thinking I would turn myself in in the morning. But the morning never came.
{{Underline|ffffff}}
[[File:Entitis.png|link=]]
==
BLoOd!
== Those present during a █████ manifestation by U.S. will encounter a human dressed in casual clothing, wielding the gun that U.S. has most recently left a review on. Stars may be present in the sky and create constellations resembling different types of guns. If one ever encounters the human during the █████ manifestation, the human will engage on those who have encountered it. The human will not de-manifest until it has killed off anyone who has witnessed it or has witnessed it killing another wanderer. It will also de-manifest if it doesn't spot anyone for a one-hour timespan. The human created by U.S. is presumably invincible and cannot be wounded. The only time it has been seen with wounds is during its attacks on dangerous entities. Other than that, it is unknown if it can be injured via traditional means. The event in which the human manifests only occurs during U.S.'s engagement in threatening behavior towards individuals in the Frontrooms. Though this can happen multiple times, only one human can manifest inside the Backrooms at a time. In some cases, however, there have been times where the human would not manifest, despite U.S.'s current behavior. In some instances, the human has been reported to de-manifest before killing off anyone that has spotted it. When this happens, it will always mention The United States of America and aim the gun to its head before de-manifesting. Many believe this to be a "tribute" of sorts to America, though, because it never truly dies, others believe it to be a "false" tribute. During the human's manifestation, certain anomalies may arise. These anomalies differ from manifestation, but always share the property of allowing the human to approach its victims quicker than normal. These anomalies have also become more and more apparent and more frequent the more times the human manifests.
















































Death caused by U.S. can be described as a state where the human consciousness does not fully disappear. Although brain scans show brain death and the fact that neurons cannot make new connections, the state in which the person was in when they died still remains. Following death by U.S., their consciousness will transfer over to U.S., who will then inherit their memories and personality.


----

That day, everything changed.

I woke up from my slumber.

I saw a man.

A man like any other.

Only he was wielding a gun.

And he headed straight towards me.


{{Underline|ffffff}}

I was scared.

But, at the same time, I felt liberated.

Had they finally found me?

I opened my arms and accepted my fate.


{{Underline|ffffff}}

I know that I'm not free.

I know that I'm not good.

I know that I should be dead.

I know that I shouldn't even be alive.

But I am.

As he gets closer to me,

I wave hello and goodbye.

Both at the same time.

At that was all it took.


{{Underline|ffffff}}

But then, I realized something.

I was never in a house at all.

I was never a bad person.

I was only...

Vermin


{{Underline|ffffff}}


















All is alive and well. Officer.





























































































> The vermin walks the roads to the abyss
> It was only as meaningful as a Pine's Job
